title: X–Y Unit Planner — Standard Node File
description: Schema for a single ACARA standard node map file. One file per standard, stored in data/.

year_level.description: Year level this standard belongs to. e.g. '7'

title.description: Short title for the standard. e.g. 'Classification and Biodiversity'

prior_knowledge.description: What students are assumed to know before this unit begins (ACARA assumed prior for this year level).

y_goal.description: Fixed conceptual endpoint for all students — the shared assessment target. Must not be exceeded.

nodes.description: Ordered sequence of conceptual waypoints from assumed prior to Y-goal. All nodes are mandatory.

id.description: Sequential node number within this standard.

label.description: Short display name for the node. e.g. 'Purpose of classification'

y_description.description: Conceptual position reached at this node — framed as 'Students can...'. This is the Y-axis position.

xmin.description: Minimum construction required before advancing to the next node. Non-negotiable floor — certifies readiness.

width_core.description: X+ task — same concept as Xmin, broader integration and coordination. Standard construction for typical friction class.

width_enrich_options.description: X++ options — open-access transfer and application beyond taught examples. Drawn from ACARA elaborations. Teachers choose one or more.

hinge.description: True if this is a hinge concept — a node where misconceptions cluster, later nodes depend on it, and width investment here pays off disproportionately.

hinge_reason.description: Required if hinge is true. Explains why this node is a hinge — dependency, misconception gateway, assessment sensitivity. Null if hinge is false.

inquiry_links.description: ACARA Science Inquiry skill codes relevant to this node. e.g. ['AC9S7I01', 'AC9S7I04']

endeavour_links.description: ACARA Science as a Human Endeavour codes relevant to this node. e.g. ['AC9S7H02']

width_terminology.Xmin: Minimum construction — non-negotiable floor before advancing
width_terminology.X+: Extended width — same concept, broader integration (width_core field)
width_terminology.X++: Synthetic width — open-access transfer beyond taught examples (width_enrich_options field)

_notes.friction_effect: Friction level determines which width level is recommended per node — it does not change the node sequence or Y-goal.
_notes.file_naming: Files should follow the pattern: {year_level}_{subject}_{code}.json e.g. y7_science_AC9S7U01.json
_notes.adding_standards: To add a new standard, create a new file in data/ following this schema. utils.py loads all files from data/ automatically.
